rulings.Case Title: PORTSIDE HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION VS. SF OAKLAND BAY LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 5, 2024 line 1. Petitioner's Motion to Confirm Arbitration is continued to September 13, 2024. No later than September 6, 2024 moving party to file a response to respondent's objection to service of Notice of Motion for Confirmation. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Release Of Property From Mechanic'S Lien [Cal. Civil Code 8460 And 8480]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 5, 2024 line 2. Petitioner's Motion for Release of Property from Mechanic's Lien is continued to October 4, 2024. No later than September 20, 2024 moving party to (1) file a request for judicial notice attaching a copy of the judgment referenced in section C of the motion and a copy of the complaint in the case resulting in such judgment; and (2) supplemental brief, not to exceed 2 pages, providing authorities for electronic service of this motion on a party that has not yet appeared in this action. Authorities shall include citations to code of civil procedure, California rules of Court, local rules and case citations. Moving party is further ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of its filings in support of the motion no later than September 20, 2024 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LINDA AZAR, TRUSTEE OF THE JULIETTE DABIS VS. MOUNER TIACH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 5, 2024 line 7. Defendant's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is GRANTED without leave to amend. The operative notice contains language indicating payment was required to be made in person rather than by mail. Unlike in Hsieh, the Notice is not silent regarding the option to pay by mail. This option is listed along with personal deliver, direct deposit and wire transfer, however only personal delivery is "checked" as a permissible option for payment, excluding the other 3 listed, by "unchecked" possibilities. Hsieh v. Pederson (2018) 23 Cal.App.5th Supp. 1. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. Failure to comply in the future will result in motion being treated as unopposed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DAQIN MEI VS. GUANGZHI ZHU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 5, 2024 line 9. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith (1) that Plaintiff did not exercise its option to inquire regarding defendants' protected status via notice of termination of tenancy; and (2) if Plaintiff so alleges, to explain the inclusion of the warning language and select information regarding protected status in the notice, i.e. if it was not an exercise of Plaintiff's option, why was this language included. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
